champion.narrative: No buyer-side champion is confirmed. A person named 'Mariana' appears in the 15 Jun 2026 note as the contact Grace Kim planned to check in with for IDB updates, but her role, title, and advocacy are unconfirmed and she may be a BCT-side or coordination contact rather than an IDB sponsor. Digesh from PwC is a reference-providing ally, not an internal IDB champion. competition.narrative: IDB explicitly told Zycus it is one of two finalists (5/11/26 note), so there is exactly one competing finalist, but that competitor is unnamed in every source and Zycus has no competitive intelligence on it. The incumbent or current-state solution at IDB is also unrecorded. Zycus is competing partially blind: without knowing the rival, the wedge cannot be tailored. The wedge that does exist is the iContract plus Merlin AI-enabled CLM story and a completed PwC reference; the move is to have BCT surface who the other finalist is and what IDB liked about each, then sharpen the Merlin/AI differentiation accordingly.

identify_pain.narrative: No explicit pain statement from IDB is captured in any source, and there are no Avoma calls to attribute pain to a named buyer. The inferred pain is contract-lifecycle management modernization at a development finance institution, consistent with the iContract scope and a formal RFP, but the operational or financial driver IDB itself articulated is unknown. The pain owner is also unconfirmed. Surfacing IDB's stated driver (compliance, contract turnaround, audit risk) through BCT is required to anchor the value story for the EB.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partially visible: the MSA was completed with BCT on 24 Apr 2026 and sent to IDB, with IDB feedback still outstanding as of 30 Jun 2026 (~67 days). Earlier, on 15 Dec 2025, the partner requested help with representations and warranties, showing legal terms were already in play. The forcing function is the 14 Jul 2026 close, which is not credible if MSA feedback, redlines, signature and PO issuance must all still happen. Visibility into IDB legal/procurement is blocked by the partner channel; getting BCT to surface the specific MSA objections would create the missing clarity.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified or engaged after roughly 7.5 months and a $100k deal. Account-level contacts include a CFO (Orlando Ferreira) and an IT Managing Director (Julio Cesar Lima), but neither is proven to be involved in this opportunity's scope and there are no opportunity contact roles on record (0 buyer-side contact roles). Budget authority and C-level sponsorship are unmapped, and because BCT runs all buyer access, Zycus cannot validate who signs. Mapping the EB through BCT, and ideally a direct introduction, is a ranked move before contracting.

decision_process.narrative: The process is reconstructable from the Next Step log: RFP, down-selection to two finalists, demo/PoC, on-site session, pricing/BAFO (best and final offer sent ~24 Mar 2026), MSA completion with BCT (24 Apr 2026), a PwC reference call (~18 May 2026), and a final vendor decision now expected early-to-mid July 2026. The buyer is running a structured two-finalist selection driven through partner BCT. The 24 Jun 2026 note attributes the latest slip to 'internal agendas/team vacations', a credible but un-forcing reason. The gap is that Zycus sees this process only second-hand through BCT; there is no direct line to the IDB decision committee or named approval chain.

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are inferred from the procurement motion, not documented from the buyer's words. IDB ran a formal RFP (received 10 Nov 2025, submitted by Zycus 11/24/25 per Next Step), down-selected on 30 Dec 2025, held a demo/PoC with the BCT team on 15 Jan 2026, ran an on-site session on 2 Mar 2026, refined user definitions and pricing, and requested a PwC reference call completed around 18 May 2026. That sequence shows criteria weighted on functional fit (CLM via iContract), user-count-based commercials, and proof via a named reference, but the specific scored requirements were never captured in Salesforce and no call exists to confirm them. Confirm the formal evaluation scorecard through BCT.

flags: Partner dependency risk: zero direct buyer relationships; entirely reliant on BCT for engagement, intelligence, and decision news. The deal has gone dark for 40+ days with no BCT update. Immediate action: escalate to Michael McCarthy (deal owner's manager) to press BCT for decision status and request a direct Zycus-to-IDB executive introduction. If BCT cannot deliver visibility in 7 days, bypass the partner and reach out directly to the IDB project lead or economic buyer (likely the Managing Director IT or CFO at account level).

No Economic Buyer mapped or engaged after 7+ months: the deal advanced through RFP, finalist selection, MSA, and reference validation without a documented commercial conversation with the IDB budget authority. This creates closure risk and explains the post-close-date silence. Immediate action: work with BCT to identify the EB (likely CFO Orlando Ferreira or Managing Director IT Julio Cesar Lima from account contacts) and request an introduction. If BCT resists, escalate to executive connect (Michael McCarthy) for a peer-to-peer EB validation call.

No Economic Buyer or champion mapped after 7.5 months. The deal has reached MSA stage with $100k contract value but no C-level or VP-level sponsor is identified or engaged, and no internal champion is advocating for Zycus. This is a critical power gap in a two-vendor finalist bake-off. Recommended action: in the IDB re-engagement call, ask who controls the budget and decision, request a direct conversation with them, and identify a project-level champion who can provide competitive intel and coach the close.

Competing blind in a two-vendor finalist race. The other finalist is unnamed and unknown — no competitive intelligence was gathered during the entire cycle. Without knowing the competitor, we cannot counter their positioning, tailor the wedge, or anticipate their win strategy. The 44-day silence may indicate the other finalist is winning. Recommended action: ask IDB directly in the re-engagement call who the other finalist is, how they are comparing the two solutions, and what would make Zycus the preferred choice. Use that intelligence to craft the final close push.

Map and activate the champion immediately: Mariana's role and advocacy are unconfirmed after 7.5 months. In a two-finalist race with no champion driving internal urgency, the other vendor may have stronger advocacy and be winning by default. If Mariana is the champion, validate her access to the EB and her willingness to push the decision; if not, identify and develop the real internal advocate before the window closes.
